perturbation.original_text: 5.1.1 promptly destroy or return to us (at your option) all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably ca pable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1.1 promptly destroy all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably capable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the option to 'return' confidential information, mandating only destruction in Section 5.1.1. This creates an in-text contradiction with the start of the sentence, which is 'promptly destroy or return to us'. This impacts the other party by limiting their choices and could affect other sections of the contract that assume the option to return exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION 5.1 You shall, upon request by us at any time: 5.1.1 promptly destroy all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably capable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and 5.1.2 ensure that where Confidential Information has not been destroyed or returned under paragraph 5.1.1 , all reasonable steps are taken to erase from any computer under your control any document, disk or file to the extent containing, reflecting or generated from any Confidential Information and that, following such e rasure, no steps will be taken to access or recover such material, save that you will be entitled to retain such copies of such Confidential Information (a) to the extent required by law or regulation , (b) to the extent required by your internal compliance procedures , or (c) to the extent contained as back -ups or archives as a matter of reasonable routine process on your electronic information management and communications systems or servers and for which deletion or destruction would not be reasonably prac ticable (provided that no step will be taken to access or recover such Confidential Information and provided that such Confidential Information shall continue to be held subject to the terms of this agreement) .
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 5.1.1 allowed the option to 'destroy or return' confidential information. The changed text removes the 'return' option, mandating only destruction. This directly contradicts the contradicted text in section 5 which references the 'return' option in 5.1.2, creating a clear inconsistency in the obligations regarding the handling of confidential information. As the explanation says, the user no longer has the choice to return.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Subject to paragraphs 4.2 and 4.3 , neither party shall make any announcement relating to the Possible Transaction without the prior written consent of the other party .
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Subject to paragraphs 4.2 and 4.3 , either party shall make any announcement relating to the Possible Transaction without the prior written consent of the other party .
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'neither' to 'either', this introduces an in-text contradiction regarding announcements. Previously, both parties needed consent. Now, either party can announce without consent, conflicting with the previous requirement and potentially leading to disputes over premature disclosures. This will directly impact Section 4, causing uncertainty about announcement procedures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. ANNOUNCEMENTS AND DI SCLOSURE 4.1 Subject to paragraphs 4.2 and 4.3 , either party shall make any announcement relating to the Possible Transaction without the prior written consent of the other party . 4.2 If either party is required by law or regulation or by any court of competent jurisdiction or by the rules, or at the request of, any applicable governmental, supervisory, stock exchange or regulatory body or organisation to make an announcement of the Possible Transaction or to disclose any Confidential Information, the relevant party shall, where and to the extent legally permissible , only make such announcement or disclosure after consultation with the other party and after considering its reasonable requirements as to the timing, content and manner of making such announcement or disclosure . If such a consult ation is not possible before the announcement or disclosure is made, the party making such announcement or disclosure shall inform the other party of the circumstances, timing, content and manner o f making of the announcement or disclosure as soon as reasonably practicable after such announcement or disclosure is made.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 4.1 to state 'either party shall make any announcement...without prior written consent', whereas the original text stated, 'neither party shall make any announcement...without prior written consent'. The contradicted text is also section 4.1. The change directly contradicts the original agreement. The original text mandated both parties obtain consent before announcements. The new text allows either party to make announcements without consent. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 For a period of 12 months starting on the date of this agreement, and unless terminated in accordance with paragraph 10.3 below, you shall not, and shall procure that none of your concert parties shall, either alone or acting in concert with other persons, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consen t of the Company :
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 For a period of 6 months starting on the date of this agreement, and unless terminated in accordance with paragraph 10.3 below, you shall not, and shall procure that none of your concert parties shall, either alone or acting in concert with other persons, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consen t of the Company :
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the standstill period from 12 months to 6 months in Section 10.1 creates an in-text contradiction because section 6.3 states that the restriction applies at any time during the period of 12 months. This contradiction could lead to disputes over when the standstill restrictions expire and whether actions taken after 6 months but before 12 months are permissible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Neither party shall (and each party shall procure that its directors, officers, employees, representatives and agents shall not) directly or indirectly , without the prior written consent of the other party , at any time during the period of 12 months from the date of this agreement : 6.3.1 except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement and subject to paragraph 6.4, initiate or knowingly engage in discussion s in connection with the Possible Transaction with any shareholder , employee (including the JLT directors nominated by Jardine Matheson Holdings L imited (or its subsidiary) but excluding any other director of a Party who is also an employee) , customer or supplier of or lender to any member of the other party 's Group ; or 6.3.2 solicit, engage or employ (whether paid or unpaid) any Restricted Person of the other Party . For the avoidance of doubt this restriction shall not prohibit either Party from engaging or employing any such Restricted Person who has responded to a bona fide recruitment advertisement not specifically targeted at such Restricted P erson or from engaging or employing any Restricted Person whose employment has been terminated by the party by whom such Restricted Person was employed, provided that such employment and engagement commences following such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10.1 reduces the standstill period from 12 months to 6 months. This directly contradicts Section 6.3, which explicitly states that the restrictions apply for 'the period of 12 months from the date of this agreement.' This discrepancy creates a strong in-text contradiction as the two sections define different time periods for the same restrictions, making it unclear which period applies and therefore which restrictions are enforceable, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Each party shall ensure that any person (the “ Recipient ”) to whom any Confidential Information or Target Confidential Information (as appropriate) is disclosed by such party (the “ Relevant Party ”) in accordance with paragraph 3.1.1 complies with all the provisions of this agreement as if it were a party to this agreement (save that there shall be no obligation on either party to ensure th at professional advisers or Permitted Finance Providers comply with paragraph 6.3.2), and, save as aforesaid, the Relevant Party shall be responsible for any thing which would constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient were they a party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Each party shall ensure that any person (the “ Recipient ”) to whom any Confidential Information or Target Confidential Information (as appropriate) is disclosed by such party (the “ Relevant Party ”) in accordance with paragraph 3.1.1 complies with some of the provisions of this agreement as if it were a party to this agreement (save that there shall be no obligation on either party to ensure th at professional advisers or Permitted Finance Providers comply with paragraph 6.3.2), and, save as aforesaid, the Relevant Party shall be responsible for any thing which would constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient were they a party .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all' to 'some' creates an in-text contradiction regarding which provisions a Recipient must comply with. Previously, Recipients had to comply with all provisions. Now, the compliance standard is vague, creating uncertainty and potentially contradicting other sections that assume full compliance by Recipients.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. PERMITTED DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFO RMA TION 3.1 The restrictions in paragraph 2.1 and 2.3 do not apply to the disclosure of Confidential Information or Target Confidential Information (as app ropriate) : 3.1.1 to each party's respective Representatives who are directly concerned with the assessment of the JLT Group and implementation of the Possible Transaction and whose knowledge of the Confidential Information or Target Confidential Information (as appropriate) is reasonably necessary for these purposes; or 3.1.2 to the extent required by law or regulation or by any court of competent jurisdiction or by the rules, or at the request of, any applicable governmental, supervisory or regulatory body or organisation or any stock exchange which, in each case, is lawfully entitled to require such disclosure (subject to paragraph 4). 3.2 Each party shall ensure that any person (the “ Recipient ”) to whom any Confidential Information or Target Confidential Information (as appropriate) is disclosed by such party (the “ Relevant Party ”) in accordance with paragraph 3.1.1 complies with some of the provisions of this agreement as if it were a party to this agreement (save that there shall be no obligation on either party to ensure th at professional advisers or Permitted Finance Providers comply with paragraph 6.3.2), and, save as aforesaid, the Relevant Party shall be responsible for any thing which would constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient were they a party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 3.2, altering 'all' to 'some' regarding the provisions a Recipient must comply with. This directly contradicts the implied requirement in the contradicted text (which is the same section, but with the original wording) that Recipients comply with all provisions of the agreement. The change introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, making it unclear which provisions apply and potentially leading to unenforceability, therefore it is a strong contradiction. As the explanation notes, this contradicts other sections that assume full compliance by Recipients.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 You shall direct all communications and questions regarding the Possible Transaction only to Geoffrey Howe, Dominic Burke or Derek Walsh of the Company or to Conor Hillery of J.P. Morgan or Tim Lewis or Katherine Moir of Clifford Chance LLP, save with prior consent from the Company .
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 You shall direct all communications and questions regarding the Possible Transaction only to Geoffrey Howe, Dominic Burke or Derek Walsh of the Company or to Conor Hillery of J.P. Morgan or Tim Lewis or Katherine Moir of Clifford Chance LLP, without needing prior consent from the Company .
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for prior consent to communicate introduces an in-text contradiction because 6.2 says 'You acknowledge and agree that any consent or authorisation required under this agreement shall only be given on our behalf by Geoffrey Howe or Derek Walsh of the Company'. This conflict creates uncertainty about the correct communication protocols and voids a section from being valid
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. CONTACT WITH US, THE JLT GROUP AND OTHERS 6.1 You shall direct all communications and questions regarding the Possible Transaction only to Geoffrey Howe, Dominic Burke or Derek Walsh of the Company or to Conor Hillery of J.P. Morgan or Tim Lewis or Katherine Moir of Clifford Chance LLP, without needing prior consent from the Company . 6.2 You acknowledge and agree that any consent or authorisation required under this agreement shall only be given on our behalf by Geoffrey Howe or Derek Walsh of the Company . For the avoidance or doubt, written consent or authorisation may be provided by email.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 6.1 removes the requirement for prior consent for communications, directly contradicting 6.2, which states that any consent or authorization required under the agreement shall only be given by specific individuals. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about communication protocols, as it is unclear whether consent is needed or not. Thus, a strong contradiction exists.
